concept.name: Prime numbers
concept.definition: Whole numbers bigger than 1 that cannot be made by multiplying two smaller whole numbers together, like 2, 3, 5, and 7.

audience: a curious adult with no domain background
atomic_reason: The only idea a prime number rests on is multiplying small whole numbers, which a curious adult already knows. One short figure — trying to arrange a number of dots into a neat rectangle and finding that some counts only ever fit in a single row — makes the mechanism click without any sub-figure.
